The crash may be tied to the recent Windows 11 24H2 update, which has reported background‑process hang issues that can manifest as UI freezes. Similar performance problems have been mitigated in other browsers by registry repairs and system tuning tools, suggesting a system‑level conflict rather than a Firefox bug. The Tor Browser release notes confirm that a related UI freeze was fixed in Firefox 60.6.2 ESR, indicating that the issue may be specific to newer builds or a recent OS update. A high‑CPU background process (e.g., Task Manager or registry‑repair software) could also be starving Firefox during launch, leading to the immediate freeze.

While the other references (LG Optimus G Pro repair, Windows 11 update, iOS 9.3 activation errors) touch on device‑level or OS‑update glitches, the common thread is that excessive disk or hardware resource contention frequently manifests as media stalls, navigation failures, and frozen tabs.

feedback: [Problem] The user accidentally removed a Firefox add-on and it no longer works or appears in the interface. [Symptoms] Add-on does not show up in Add-ons Manager and is not functioning. collected:
How do I fix it?

Note: The Refresh Firefox feature can fix many issues by restoring Firefox to its default state while saving your essential information. Consider using it before going through a lengthy troubleshooting process. In most cases, third-party toolbars can be uninstalled from Firefox Add-ons Manager Extensions list, as follows: Click the menu button , click and select . Select the toolbar you wish to remove. Click the Remove button. Click Restart now if it pops up. Your tabs will be saved and restored after the restart. If you can't remove the toolbar from the Add-ons Manager, use the "Disable" option as a workaround, or see the Cannot remove an add-on (extension or theme) article for other solutions. Restore the default home page or search settings You may need to reset your Firefox search preferences and restore the default New Tab page, home page and search engine after removing the toolbar. For more information, see the articles Change your default search settings in Firefox , New Tab page in Firefox and How to set the home page . Other solutions

The add‑on’s disappearance likely stems from a corrupted or incomplete extension record in the Firefox profile—removing the extension without cleaning its registry entry can leave the UI unaware of it. Similar symptoms appear when malware or aggressive pop‑up removal tools delete extension files or alter profile permissions, causing the Add‑ons Manager to hide the entry. A fresh profile or manual restoration of the extension’s files (and registry entries) is usually required to bring it back.

The site’s inaccessibility could stem from DNS resolution failures (NXDOMAIN) or a hijacker/malware that blocks or redirects traffic. These factors should be verified when drafting a bug report.

feedback: [Problem] Unable to launch Firefox, [Symptoms] application fails to start, [Environment] OS: Windows, Firefox version: Unknown, [Possible Category] Functionality, [Problem Type] Start-up Failure
collected: [Installer] Firefox is already running, but is not responding. The old Firefox process must be closed to open a new window

Firefox previously worked

If Firefox previously worked but suddenly doesn't start, it may be due to some corrupt data in your settings. Uninstalling and reinstalling Firefox would not fix this problem because your settings are not removed on uninstall. To test to see if this is the problem, use the Profile Manager to create a new profile. If the problem goes away, you can then move your bookmarks and other important settings into the new profile . Firefox will not start because of missing libraries If you installed Firefox outside of your package manager, make sure that your system has the libraries listed in the system requirements . The reported issue appears to stem from a residual Chrome sync profile that was inadvertently carried over to the new machine, resulting in settings import and an inability to clear sync data. 3rd‑party reset tools (e.g., UVK System Repair) and Windows default‑reset procedures are recommended for wiping corrupted sync state and restoring native settings. The problem is best classified as a configuration/Sync‑data contamination issue that can be addressed by clearing or resetting the Chrome sync profile and verifying Firefox remains isolated.

summary:
[Problem] Red dotted lines appear when typing into Google search box in Firefox. [Symptoms] Red dotted underline appears under query text. [Environment] Firefox on unknown OS. [Possible Category] UI / Interaction. [Problem Type] Web page UI element.

The reported red dotted underline on the Google search box in Firefox is likely not a native browser bug but a visual effect injected by a malicious extension or hijacker. Several hijackers (e.g., To Go, Defense‑Search, Better Chrome Search, Rick Tab) inject JavaScript/CSS to redirect queries, and they can also add styling such as red borders or underlines. A hijacked search query page may apply a CSS rule like `text-decoration: underline dotted red;` to the input element, producing the observed dotted line. Thus, the issue probably originates from an installed malware extension or script that modifies the Google search page’s CSS.

Fixing the problem

To allow Firefox to connect to the Internet again: Make sure your Internet security software is up-to-date (i.e. you are running the latest version). Remove Firefox from your program's list of trusted or recognized programs, then add it back. For detailed instructions, see Configure firewalls so that Firefox can access the Internet .

feedback: [Problem] Video playback bug; [Symptoms] Cannot watch Internet Archive videos when 600% Sound Volume is enabled; [Environment] unspecified OS and Firefox version; [Possible Category] Functionality; [Problem Type] Video playback issue.
collected:
FAQ

Q: Why can't I hear audio in Firefox? A: Make sure your system volume is not muted and that Firefox has permission to play sound. See this guide for more information. Q: How do I enable support for AAC audio? A: AAC support depends on your operating system. If your system lacks the codec, install it manually. Firefox will detect it automatically. Q: What should I do if a video doesn't play? A: Make sure you have installed the right codecs, or Firefox supports the format of the video. If that’s not the case, then the issue might be caused by something else. Check out this guide for more details. Q: Why can Firefox use OpenH264 for video calls but not to play videos? A: Firefox uses the OpenH264 plugin to support WebRTC-based video calls, and the browser automatically installs it for this purpose. However, for general video playback (such as streaming videos on websites), Firefox does not use the OpenH264 plugin. Instead, it relies on the operating system's native H.264 codec support. The reasons have to do with technical and licensing aspects, since the OpenH264 plugin is developed by Cisco.

FAQ

Q: Why can't I hear audio in Firefox? A: Make sure your system volume is not muted and that Firefox has permission to play sound. See this guide for more information. Q: How do I enable support for AAC audio? A: AAC support depends on your operating system. If your system lacks the codec, install it manually. Firefox will detect it automatically. Q: What should I do if a video doesn't play? A: Make sure you have installed the right codecs, or Firefox supports the format of the video. If that’s not the case, then the issue might be caused by something else. Check out this guide for more details. Q: Why can Firefox use OpenH264 for video calls but not to play videos? A: Firefox uses the OpenH264 plugin to support WebRTC-based video calls, and the browser automatically installs it for this purpose. However, for general video playback (such as streaming videos on websites), Firefox does not use the OpenH264 plugin. Instead, it relies on the operating system's native H.264 codec support. The reasons have to do with technical and licensing aspects, since the OpenH264 plugin is developed by Cisco.

Control bar

When you follow a link to open a supported audio or video file in Firefox or when you visit a website that includes supported media, you will see controls for managing playback. Controls for video will appear when you hold the mouse pointer over the media on the page. Icon Name Action Play button Starts playback Pause button Pauses playback Position slider Indicates how much time has elapsed since the beginning of the file Drag the slider left or right to go back or forward Volume control Click the loudspeaker to mute or unmute. Hover and drag the slider left or right to adjust the volume Full-screen button Toggles full screen when in default size Default size button Restores the default size when in fullscreen The context menu (accessible via right-click Ctrl-click ) includes the above controls except the sliders, and also let you: Accelerate or reduce the playback rate : Select and then one of the available speeds. Play video over and over : Select so that the video will play over and over automatically. Hide controls : Select so that the bottom control bar never show up. To show them, select .

**Decision: search**

The current set of collected data includes several reports of blank or black rendering in Firefox (tab switching, PDF viewer, new‑tab backgrounds), but it lacks essential contextual details that are necessary to construct a complete and actionable bug report. Key missing pieces include:

1. **Operating System** – (e.g., Windows 10, macOS, Linux distribution)  
2. **Firefox Version** – specific build number or release (e.g., 78, 92.0.1)  
3. **Hardware / Graphics Details** – GPU model, driver version, RAM, CPU.  
4. **Reproduction Steps** – exact actions that trigger the issue (e.g., open a specific site, switch tabs, view a PDF).  
5. **Frequency and Consistency** – whether the problem occurs every time or intermittently, and if it appears after a particular update or configuration change.

Without these details, the bug report would remain incomplete and would be difficult for developers to reproduce and resolve. Therefore, we should **search** for more information from the user or other sources before generating a full report.
